title: Performance regression in Skrooge
description:
drh added on 2013-05-06 21:05:17:
A Skrooge developer reports that the latest SQLite gives a performance
regression on one query.  A test script that demonstrates the change
in the query plan between check-in [82eb7eadb8c76b3a] and
check-in [3f87f4593b631819] is attached.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, value FLOAT DEFAULT 0.0, created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (name, value) VALUES ('Test A', 10.5);
INSERT INTO test_table (name, value) VALUES ('Test B', NULL);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL ORDER BY created_at DESC;
UPDATE test_table SET value = value + 1 WHERE id = 1;
key_bug_features: Performance regression on a specific SQLite query
